response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine alignment with the AI-native engineering requirement through his current Staff role at re:cinq, where he contributes to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, and he has real high-throughput distributed systems experience from Dunnhumby, where he worked on micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second. However, Python — a must-have per the JD — is entirely absent from the resume, skills section, and project history, which is a significant red flag. TypeScript is well-represented. Staff-level titles are consistent across his career, but explicit evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, on-call ownership, and incident-response capability is thin. A screening call to probe Python proficiency and operational experience is warranted before progressing.
strengths: Current role at re:cinq directly involves AI-agent infrastructure and agentic workflow tooling, matching the AI-native engineering baseline the JD treats as non-negotiable
Proven high-throughput distributed systems background at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency micro-services architecture
Consistent Staff-level titles and IC technical leadership across multiple companies, with demonstrated TDD, CI/CD, and agile practice at RIDE Capital
concerns: Python does not appear anywhere on the resume, in the skills list, or in any project description, despite being a must-have skill in the JD
No explicit mention of on-call rotations, incident response ownership, or SLA-level operational accountability in any role
Current AI-native role started April 2025 (approximately one month at time of resume), providing a very limited track record in that context
